lever.description: For supported segments whose primitives are only Add/Mul, run a cached Add/Mul-only rewrite slice containing the same applicable Add/Mul rules in their original relative order; all other segments continue using the full cached rule set.

proof: Rule membership is restricted only when the segment language contains Add, Mul, literals, and variables; excluded full-rule entries cannot match because their LHS roots are absent and the retained Add/Mul rules do not introduce those roots.
Retained Add/Mul rule order matches the full aggressive rule relative order: add-comm, mul-comm, add-assoc, mul-assoc, add-zero, mul-one, factor, mul-zero.
The existing aggressive benchmark golden fingerprint is unchanged.
Eval samples x=-2.0, 0.0, 2.0, 7.5 match original and optimized outputs.
No operand ordering, tie-breaking, RNG, or additional floating-point reassociation surface is introduced beyond the existing aggressive Add/Mul rules.
